description: In an apartment living room, Joey and Ross stand while Frank sits in a black recliner. Chandler opens the front door and enters. An unidentified off-screen voice says, "Can play."
Chandler closes the door behind him, holding a basketball in one hand. Joey turns his head to watch him.
"Oh, Joe. You left your keys in the door. Here you go," Chandler says, tossing a set of keys to Joey, who catches them as audience laughter is audible.
Joey looks down at the keys, then turns toward Ross and Frank, tossing the keys onto a surface. Chandler bounces the basketball once, the impact echoing clearly.
Joey steps closer to Ross, extending one hand toward Frank in the recliner as Ross looks on.
"The point is," Joey says, lifting his hands.
Joey keeps his hands raised in front of him.
"there's a lot of women out there you haven't even had sex with yet," Joey says, moving his hands outward.
"Yeah, he's right. He's right," Ross says, gesturing toward Joey.
"This is your time," Ross points down with one finger.
Ross lowers his hand.
"You're young, you're weird," Ross points at Frank, then shrugs his shoulders.
"Chicks dig that," Ross says, crossing his arms over his chest.
"Okay, but isn't sex better when it's with one person that you really, really care about?" Frank asks, lifting his hands from the armrests.
"Yeah, in a poem," Ross replies, directing his gaze downward.
"maybe," Ross adds.
Joey shakes his head from side to side slightly while audience laughter plays.
"The man's right. That's what I had with Rachel," Joey gestures to Frank, then turns his head toward Ross.
"You don't have it anymore?" Frank asks. "No," Ross replies.
Ross maintains his gaze downward.
"I slept with someone else," Ross says, still looking down.
"Okay, so wait. All right, so how did that make things better?" Frank sits up slightly, moving his hands upward as he asks.
Ross shifts his posture slightly.
"It didn't," Ross states, shaking his head.
Joey touches his nose with one hand, looking downward.
"Okay, so what you used to have with Rachel is what I got with Alice," Frank says, leaning forward in the chair.
"Now, what is that like?" Joey asks, stepping closer and pointing at Frank.
"It's so cool, man," Frank replies with a visible smile.
"It's just because being with her is so much better than not being with her," Frank explains, gesturing with both hands.
Joey keeps his eyes on Frank.
"Why can't I find that?" Joey points a finger to his own chest.
"Don't ask me. I had it and I blew it," Ross answers, gesturing with his hands as audience laughter sounds.
"Why want it?" Joey points to Ross. "You can have it," Frank says rapidly, gesturing at Joey.
"I don't know. Maybe I can. I mean, maybe there's something wrong with me," Joey says, touching his chest again.
Frank pushes himself up from the recliner.
"No, it's out there, man," Frank says, standing and stepping toward Joey.
"I've seen it. I got it," Frank taps his chest with one hand.
Joey points an index finger forward.
"Then you hold on to right, man," Joey says, pointing firmly at Frank.
"Congratulations," Joey steps forward and wraps his arms around Frank.
Joey and Frank remain in an embrace while Ross watches.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen where Joey sits on the counter next to Ross. "You're Frank's best man," Phoebe says, standing nearby.
Joey leans back with his arms crossed while Ross eats from a bowl of chips on his lap.
"I couldn't help it. Their love is so pure," Joey states, opening his arms briefly.
Phoebe opens her mouth to speak.
"Well, what about, huh?" Phoebe asks, turning her gaze toward Ross.
"I'm the ring bearer," Ross states, pointing a chip toward his chest before eating it amid audience laughter.
Phoebe stands near the counter. Chandler walks out of an adjacent room wearing a red towel around his waist and a pink shower cap.
Chandler walks past the group and continues out of view. Phoebe raises one hand slightly as he passes, and audience laughter is audible.
Joey tilts his head sideways, maintaining his gaze in the direction Chandler walked.
The setting changes to an exterior view of a city street lined with brick buildings, where a few vehicles and pedestrians move along the road.
The setting changes to an apartment interior. "Hi. Oh, Alice. Hi. Thanks," Phoebe says, standing by the open door as Alice enters.

"I'm so glad you could come because I've got a real home EC emergency. Oh, my God. Who died on this?" Phoebe walks toward a round table, and Alice approaches to look at a large yellow stain on a pink tablecloth.
Alice bends slightly to inspect the stain.
"It's a real mustardastrophe," Phoebe says, standing beside the table as audience laughter sounds.
Alice looks closely at the yellow stain on the tablecloth.
"Can you help me?" Phoebe asks, gesturing toward the table.
"Absolutely," Alice nods.
"Okay, first, we'll start with a little club soda and salt. And then if that doesn't work, then we can go back to" Alice touches the fabric near the stain as she speaks.
"you know what? Forget it." Phoebe pulls a section of the tablecloth toward herself.
"Ruined," Phoebe says, letting go of the cloth amid audience laughter.
"Oh, no. Never say that. If you can't get it out, then you can cut around the stain add a little lace. You make a stylish throw," Alice lifts the stained area and smiles.
Phoebe stares at Alice without moving her facial expression.
"Or instead, maybe you could just not marry my brother Frank," Phoebe speaks, her gaze fixed on Alice, followed by audience laughter.
Alice's smile fades as she looks at Phoebe. The setting changes to tall glass skyscrapers extending upward under a dusky sky.

The setting changes to a spacious office. "Okay, that's great. But can we make it smaller? Can we make it fit on the head of a pin? I love when we make things" Pete paces while holding a remote control, speaking toward three small television screens displaying people's faces.
"fit on the head of a pin. Got it. Yeah, okay," Pete finishes. The man on the right screen responds, "Got it." Pete replies.
An intercom beeps audibly.
"You have a Ms. Monica Geller here? Absolutely. Yeah, send her in. Hi." A voice speaks over the intercom. Pete responds, turning toward the double doors. The doors open and Monica enters.
Monica walks into the office, holding up a small rectangular slip of paper.
"What the hell is this?" Monica asks, extending the paper forward. Pete holds up an index finger toward her.
"Hang on for a SEC. I'll talk to you in the morning." Pete points the remote at the screens. The man on the right screen says, "You got it." "I'm" Pete says, turning back to Monica.
"sorry. What? Seriously? What is this supposed to mean?" Pete stands before Monica, who gestures with the slip of paper.
Pete looks at the slip of paper in Monica's hand.
"Well, you know, I never know how much to tip," Pete shrugs, his hands open.
Monica lowers her hands, keeping her eyes steadily on Pete.
"You're supposed to double the tax, not double the tax of Romania," Monica says, making emphatic gestures with the paper as audience laughter is audible.
Pete stands with his hands clasped over the remote control in front of his waist.
"What's the deal? Are you trying to buy me? Is this the way you get girls to go out with you?" Monica asks, her hands moving outward.
Pete shifts his gaze toward the television screens on the wall.
"I'm still here," the man on the right screen says.
Pete points the remote at the screens, which turn to black.
In an office with large windows overlooking a city, Pete stands near a desk while Monica stands facing him.
Pete gestures with his hands as he approaches Monica. "You're taking this all wrong. Because if I didn't leave you that tip, you want to come down here. We won't be having this argument"
Pete continues, "and there won't be this heat between us." Monica responds, "What?"
Pete points his thumb at himself. "Come on, you got to admit that our relationship is hitting a new level now. Because you used to be, like, the chef and I was the"
Pete continues to gesture with his hands.
Pete points both thumbs toward himself and then outward. "customer. But now we're like this couple that fights"
Monica looks down at a small piece of paper in her hands.
Monica raises one hand with her palm facing out, saying, "you're alone."
Pete lowers his hands and steps closer to Monica.
"Look, forget the check, okay?" Pete says.
"I like you," Pete says, nodding slightly.
Pete maintains eye contact with Monica.
"I think you're great," Pete adds.
Monica looks at Pete.
"Come on, what do you say?" Pete asks.
Monica lowers her gaze, then looks back up at Pete.
"I don't know," Monica says. Pete asks, "Why not?"
Monica looks down again, turning her head slightly.
Monica raises her hands, palms open, and says, "I don't want to encourage this kind of behavior."
Pete smiles and raises one index finger.
Pete holds up his finger. "One meal. That's all I'm asking for. Please?"
Pete opens both hands outward.
"We go out, we eat, and if you don't have a good time, I'll give you ten grand," Pete says, gesturing with his hands.
"We call it even," Pete finishes. Both smile.
The setting changes to the exterior of a city building at night. Traffic and ambient street noise are audible as a yellow taxi drives past.
Inside a dimly lit apartment, Phoebe enters wearing a coat and carrying a bag over her shoulder while holding mail. She walks toward a small table.
Phoebe turns on a table lamp, illuminating the room, and begins sorting through the mail. Frank is visible lying on the couch, covered in clothes.
Frank raises his head slightly. "Hi," he says.
Phoebe jumps, flinging the mail into the air while shouting loudly, "No,"
Phoebe continues to keep her hands raised in surprise, while Frank sits up quickly on the couch.
Frank raises both hands, palms out. "just put the mail down. It's me," he says.
Phoebe lowers her hands and says, "Okay."
Phoebe catches her breath, looking toward Frank.
Phoebe looks toward the couch. "Sorry. Why were you just, like, all in the dark?" she asks.
Frank begins moving the clothes off himself and stands up. "Oh, well, your laundry smelled so good that I thought I'd curl up in it. Is that all right?" he asks.
Phoebe responds, "Oh, yeah,"
Phoebe unbuttons her coat. "sure. So, how was your day?" she asks.
Frank replies, "Well, this is probably the worst one since I've been"
Frank finishes removing the clothes from the couch and stands near it.
Phoebe approaches Frank, who holds his hand to the back of his head.
Frank rubs one eye with his hand. "Alice. She called it off," he says. Phoebe replies, "Oh, no."
Frank looks down, maintaining a subdued posture.
"Did she say why?" Phoebe asks.
Frank gestures with his hand and says, "No, not really. Just that I was too young. But I don't see how I could all of a sudden be"
Frank opens his arms slightly. "too young because I'm older than I was when we first got together."
Phoebe looks down, shaking her head slightly.
"Yeah. No," Phoebe says softly.
Phoebe looks back up at Frank.
Phoebe reaches out and touches Frank's forehead. "I don't know. But you know what? Maybe it's just all for the best."
Frank replies, "Yeah, the best is, like, unbelievable pain."
Phoebe wraps her arms around Frank, hugging him. Frank rests his head on her shoulder.
Still holding Phoebe, Frank says, "No, I was just finally happy for the first time in my life"
They break the hug, and Frank turns toward the dining table.
Frank approaches the table. "after my dad left me," he says.
Frank places his hands flat on the tabletop.
Frank leans over the table. "and then getting arrested for stealing those birds."
Frank climbs onto the table, positioning himself on his hands and knees.
"And then the whole punctured lung thing," Frank says as he lowers his torso toward the table.
Frank lies completely flat, face down on the table.
Resting his cheek on the table, Frank says, "It's still really hard to take deep breaths in cold weather. But with Alice, all that stuff kind of went away."
Frank turns his head slightly while continuing to lie flat.
"And now it's gone, and I don't know why," Frank continues.
Phoebe looks down at Frank, her expression steady.
"Well, I can tell you why. It's because of me," Phoebe says.
Phoebe brings both hands up to cover her face briefly.
Phoebe lowers her hands and says, "But you know what?"
"I only did it because I love you," Phoebe adds, gesturing toward him.
"Okay?" Phoebe finishes.
In an apartment, Phoebe sits at a small table, facing Frank, who leans forward resting his hands on the table. "Okay?" Phoebe says, and Frank replies, "What?"
Phoebe moves her hand near her mouth.
Phoebe gestures with one hand. "Well, I kind of had a little chat with Alice," she says.
"And I sort of made her see why you two shouldn't be together," Phoebe adds, gesturing with both hands.
"And you're going to see it, too, one day," Phoebe adds.
"You really, really will," Phoebe continues.
Frank stands up straight, stepping back slightly. "Wait a minute. Wait," he says.
"This is because of you?" Frank asks, pointing a finger toward her.
Frank gestures with his thumb. "Okay, wait, no, my mother didn't want us to be together," he says.
"But the worst thing she ever did was tie me to the porch," Frank adds. Audience laughter is audible.
Frank lowers his head and steps back, placing a hand on his hip. Audience laughter continues.
Frank leans forward toward the table again. "You know what? I came to you because I thought you'd understand," he says.
Frank places his hands on the table and vaults over it. "Oh, no," he says.
Frank walks to the apartment door, opens it, then stops and turns back toward Phoebe. Audience laughter is audible.
Frank stands in the open doorway, facing Phoebe. "I would storm out of here right now," he says.
"If I had some money or a place to go," Frank adds, keeping his hands at his sides.
Phoebe looks at Frank in silence. Audience laughter continues.
The setting changes to a view of city buildings at night, then transitions to an apartment where Monica, Rachel, Ross, Joey, and Chandler are gathered.
Joey sits on a sofa next to Ross, leaning forward. "So, Ross, if you had a million dollars, what's the first thing you'd buy?" Joey asks.
Ross sits back on the sofa with his arms crossed. "I'd probably get some municipal bonds and then put the rest of the money in the bank and live off the interest," he replies.
Chandler, seated on a coffee table, looks at Ross. "Well, we've heard from Dr. Fun," Chandler says. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel sits at a dining table with Monica. Rachel claps her hands together and looks toward the window.
Rachel gasps and says, "Oh, my God."
Chandler jumps up from the coffee table, waving his hands. "The millionaires here. Oh, my God," Rachel says off-screen as Chandler cheers.
Chandler moves toward the door. Monica stands up, holding a jacket, and holds her hand up toward the others.
Monica holds up her hand. "Guys, please, I'm just going to have dinner with—" she begins.
Chandler gestures with his hand. "Okay, okay, just because he buys you dinner does not mean you owe him anything," he says.
Monica looks at Chandler and nods. Audience laughter is audible.
"I know," Monica says.
Chandler points a finger at Monica.
"Okay, then get the lobster," Chandler says.
Monica turns to the door and opens it. Pete stands in the hallway. Audience laughter is audible.
"Hey," Pete says. Monica replies, "Hi."
Ross, Chandler, and Joey, standing behind Monica, say in unison, "Hey," as they wave.
Pete smiles at the group. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey asks Pete, "How much cash you got in your pocket right now?"
Pete laughs as Joey points at him. Audience laughter continues.
Monica looks back at the group. "And that's why I'm not inviting you in for a drink," she says.
"Bye," Monica says to the group.
Monica steps into the hallway, pulling the door shut behind her. She looks at Pete.
Pete and Monica walk down the hallway. "Where do you want to go? You like pizza?" Pete asks. "Oh, that sounds great," Monica replies.
"I know a great little place," Pete adds. The setting changes to a night view of the Colosseum in Rome. A male singer's voice begins, "When the moon hits your".
The setting changes to Monica and Pete sitting at a restaurant table. The male singer's voice continues, "eye like a big pizza pie, that's a".
Pete gestures toward Monica and says, "You're not paying for the pizza."
Monica holds her wallet. "Come on, it's only fair," she says.
"You paid for the flight," Monica adds.
Monica holds up a small coin.
"Is that enough?" Monica asks.
"Lira. I throw another thousand on that," Pete says, looking at Monica.
"Well, how much is that?" Monica asks. "That's about sixty cents," Pete replies.
Monica smiles as Pete speaks. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to a nighttime view of a city skyline. An off-screen speaker says, "Forty."
The setting changes to the inside of an apartment. A bedroom door opens and Joey steps out.
Joey, wearing a t-shirt and pajama pants, leans against a bookshelf and groans, "Oh."
Joey walks across the room, wrapping his arms around his waist.
Joey stops and places his hand on his chest. "Every night," he says.
Joey paces in the dim room, rubbing his chest. Audience laughter is audible.
A recorded female voice plays: "Cigarettes don't control you." Joey stops pacing and listens.
"You are a strong, confident woman," the recorded female voice continues.
"does not need to smoke," the recorded female voice continues.
"A strong, confident woman," the recorded female voice repeats. Joey lowers his hand from his chest.
"Who does not need to smoke," the voice finishes. Joey smiles.
Joey stands up straighter, lifting his chin and puffing out his chest slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey turns and walks back through the doorway. The setting changes to another apartment. Frank is asleep, his head resting on his arms on a small table in front of a television.
Phoebe opens the apartment door, steps inside, and closes the door. She looks toward Frank.
"Hey, Frank, dear," Phoebe says softly.
Frank remains asleep on the table.

In a living room, Frank rests his head on his arm atop a television showing a program. Off-screen, Phoebe says, "Look, okay, I know that you think I did this, like, totally evil thing. But I so didn't."
Frank picks at a small figurine resting on the television.
Still off-screen, Phoebe continues, "There's someone here who I think can explain this better than I can."
Near the open apartment door, Phoebe stands beside Alice.
An uncertain speaker says, "We can go look."
Alice looks toward Frank as audience laughter plays.
Alice says, "Hi, Frank."
Frank lifts his head quickly, pushes himself off the television, and turns around, facing Alice.
Frank says, "Hi, Mrs. Knight."
Phoebe steps further into the room, taking off her jacket. Alice steps forward.
Alice says, "Phoebe's, right, frank," her voice soft.
Alice continues speaking, her hands clasped in front of her.
Alice says, "I know it's hard to hear, but it would have been wrong to go through with it. I was being selfish."
Alice continues, "Even though we want the same things right now in the future," pacing slightly.
Alice pauses.
Alice adds, "we may not."
Frank looks at Alice, his mouth slightly open.
Frank asks, "Is that it? Is that what it is?" Alice looks back at Phoebe. Standing with her arms crossed, Phoebe replies, "Yes, but not right. Not just that."
Frank rubs the back of his head.
Alice says, "Even though we love each other as much as we do," looking at Frank.
Alice lowers her gaze.
Alice says, "nonetheless."
Frank stares at Alice, pointing his finger slightly.
Frank repeats, "nonetheless, nonetheless," gesturing with his hand.
Alice turns her head to look at Phoebe.
Phoebe says, "um," nodding slightly.
Alice turns back to Frank.
Alice says, "you're too young," holding her coat against her chest.
Frank listens, his expression still.
Alice steps closer to Frank, touching his face as she says, "to really know what you want."
Frank pulls Alice into a hug and they kiss. Phoebe says, "Right. Exactly."
Frank and Alice continue to kiss and embrace. Audience laughter plays.
Phoebe raises her hands and says, "All right. It's a goodbye kiss. That's good."
Frank lifts Alice off the ground while still kissing her. The audience laughter grows louder.
Phoebe smiles and says, "Bye bye."
Frank lowers Alice back to the floor, and they continue kissing.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "Okay. No, the important thing is that you see what I'm saying."
Frank and Alice fall back onto the sofa, continuing to kiss. Audience laughter increases.
Phoebe gestures toward them and says loudly, "This is clearly wrong."
Phoebe turns away, resting her hands on her hips.
Phoebe says, "Okay," lifting her hands.
Phoebe says, "I'm going to let this happen," walking toward the center of the room.
Phoebe pauses and looks toward the couch.
Phoebe says, "Okay."
Phoebe says, "I just get my purse," moving past the couch.
Phoebe reaches over the couch, retrieves her purse, and steps back.
Phoebe says, "Okay."
Phoebe adds, "All right. Good," as she walks toward the door.

Phoebe exits the apartment, closing the door behind her. On-screen text reads "EXECUTIVE PRODUCERS KEVIN S. BRIGHT MARTA KAUFFMAN DAVID CRANE" over the empty room. The screen cuts to black, and the setting changes to a dark bedroom where Chandler lies in bed under the covers. A recording begins to play.

A recorded male voice says, "Cigarettes don't control you. You are a strong, confident woman who does not need to smoke." Chandler rests his hands on his chest, with a small stuffed animal visible near his shoulder, while on-screen production credits continue to appear.
A click is heard from the cassette player, and the recorded voice changes to Joey's voice.
The recording of Joey says, "Joey's your best friend." Chandler smiles slightly with his eyes closed.
Chandler remains still, his eyes closed.
The recording continues, "You want to make him a cheese sandwich every day."
Chandler opens his eyes and turns his head. Audience laughter plays.
The recording adds, "And you also want to buy him hundreds of dollars worth of pants." Chandler lifts his head slightly, his brow furrowed.
Chandler sits up partially, looking toward the cassette player. Audience laughter continues. A production logo appears on-screen.
An uncertain speaker says, "Close."
The screen remains black.
The screen remains black.
